heading: Transportation of official records and papers to a Senator’s State
label: 6319 Transportation of official records and papers to a Senator’s State
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 59d–1 of this titleSection is from the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005, which is div. G of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2005.

children.heading: Payment of reasonable transportation expenses
children.label: a Payment of reasonable transportation expenses
children.text: Upon request of a Senator, amounts in the appropriation account “Miscellaneous Items” within the contingent fund of the Senate shall be available to pay the reasonable expenses of sending or transporting the official records and papers of the Senator from the District of Columbia to any location designated by such Senator in the State represented by the Senator.

children.heading: Sending and transportation
children.label: b Sending and transportation
children.text: The Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate shall provide for the most economical means of sending or transporting the official records and papers under this section while ensuring the orderly and timely delivery of the records and papers to the location specified by the Senator.

children.heading: Oversight
children.label: c Oversight
children.text: The Committee on Rules and Administration shall have the authority to issue rules and regulations to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Official records defined
children.label: d Official records defined
children.text: In this section, the term “official records and papers” means books, records, papers, and official files which could be sent as franked mail.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: This section shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2005 and each succeeding fiscal year.
